title: Tornadoes and Flash Floods Are Ruining Father's Day Across the Midwest
script: Not the Father's Day plan! A violent storm system swept the Midwest today — Father's Day — threatening millions with flash floods, tornadoes, and 90 mph winds. The soil is already soaked from last week's tornado outbreak. Rain is falling at 2 inches per hour in some spots. The Illinois, Wabash, and Mississippi rivers are all running dangerously high — there is nowhere left for water to go. This same system already raked Iowa, Kentucky, Ohio, and Wisconsin this week. Today it pushed through eastern Colorado, Nebraska, Kansas, and Missouri. Parts of the central Plains hit an extreme risk rating. Millions who planned backyard barbecues got flash-flood warnings instead. Happy Father's Day. So: how many states were actually under severe weather warnings today?
